name: Google Gemini API
description: Google's first-party developer API (AI Studio) for Gemini models

provider.comment: Google's first-party developer API for Gemini models, offered via Google AI Studio (generativelanguage.googleapis.com). Distinct from Vertex AI, which is Google Cloud's enterprise surface for the same models.

category.comment: Google trains and serves its own Gemini models over its own API.

api-launched.comment: The Gemini developer API launched via Google AI Studio in December 2023 alongside Gemini 1.0 (it succeeded the earlier PaLM/MakerSuite API). No dedicated dated source fetched; date from general product history.

status.comment: The developer API is generally available; individual newer features/models may be in preview.

auth-methods.comment: Requests authenticate with an API key from Google AI Studio. OAuth is used for some user-scoped operations, but API key is the primary developer-API auth method.

self-serve-signup.comment: A developer can obtain a working API key self-serve via Google AI Studio without sales contact.

free-credits-trial.comment: A free tier with usable rate limits exists for development before moving to pay-as-you-go (checked 2026-07-24).

regional-endpoints.comment: The developer API is a single global endpoint (generativelanguage.googleapis.com) available in 190+ countries; it does not expose per-region call endpoints. Regional selection is a Vertex AI feature.

data-residency-options.comment: The developer API / AI Studio processes on Google global infrastructure with no region pinning. Data residency guarantees require Gemini via Vertex AI (a separate candidate).

model-breadth.comment: Only Google's own model families (Gemini, plus Google specialized models like Imagen, embeddings, TTS, Lyria) are reachable.

open-weight-models.comment: The API serves Google's proprietary Gemini models. Google's open-weight Gemma models are distributed separately (Kaggle/Hugging Face/Vertex) rather than being the API's offering.

model-version-pinning.comment: Stable model IDs point to a specific snapshot that usually does not change, and dated preview IDs (e.g. gemini-2.5-flash-preview-09-2025) are available; floating -latest aliases also exist.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: The API supports fine-tuning/tuned models that are then callable through the same API.

model-catalog-size.comment: Roughly 40-50 distinct callable models across text (Gemini 2.5/3 series), image generation, TTS, music, and embeddings (checked 2026-07-24).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: generateContent provides a modern multi-turn content/messages endpoint.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Docs: the model supports calling multiple functions in a single turn (parallel function calling) as well as sequential/compositional calling.

structured-output.comment: Supports a responseSchema / structured-output config that constrains responses to a JSON schema, stronger than valid-JSON-only mode.

system-prompt.comment: A distinct system_instruction field is supported at the API level.

image-input-vision.comment: Multimodal image understanding is supported as request input.

audio-input.comment: Audio understanding (speech input) is a documented core capability.

document-pdf-input.comment: Native PDF ingestion (up to ~1000 pages) with full multimodal understanding.

image-generation.comment: Image generation/editing via Gemini image models ("Nano Banana") and Imagen through the same API.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Speech generation / TTS output is offered through the same API.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: A text-embeddings endpoint (Gemini embedding models) is available under the same API.

prompt-caching.comment: Context caching is supported (both explicit and implicit), billed at a reduced input rate plus per-hour storage.

batch-api.comment: An asynchronous Batch API is offered at roughly 50% of standard cost.

max-context-window.comment: Gemini 2.5 Pro supports ~1M input tokens. (Gemini 1.5 Pro historically offered 2M but is deprecated.) Checked 2026-07-24.

extended-reasoning.comment: Thinking/reasoning mode with configurable thinking budget / reasoning-effort levels is exposed at the API level.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party Grounding with Google Search tool is callable via the API.

built-in-code-execution.comment: First-party code execution tool (sandboxed) is available as a built-in tool.

mcp-support.comment: The Interactions API documents connecting to remote MCP servers (Streamable HTTP); SDK-level MCP tool support also exists in the Python/JS SDKs (experimental).

files-retrieval-api.comment: A Files API for upload/reference is available; managed retrieval (File Search / semantic retrieval) is also offered.

agents-assistants-api.comment: The core Gemini API does not offer an OpenAI-style stateful assistants/threads primitive; conversation state is managed by the caller. (Higher-level agent products exist separately.)

openai-compatible-api.comment: An OpenAI-compatible endpoint (base https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai/) lets OpenAI SDK code run with a base-URL swap; documented as beta.

official-sdks.comment: Google Gen AI SDK is offered in Python (google-genai), JavaScript/TypeScript (@google/genai), Go, and Java.

native-api-style.comment: The native request/response shape is Google's own generateContent format (contents/parts), distinct from OpenAI or Anthropic message shapes.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch jobs are polled for completion; no documented webhook/callback delivery.

published-sla.comment: No contractual uptime SLA is published for the AI Studio developer API; an enterprise SLA is available via Gemini on Vertex AI / Google Cloud.

rate-limit-model.comment: Fixed per-tier RPM/TPM caps (Free / Tier 1 / 2 / 3), with higher tiers unlocked by cumulative spend.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: First-party single-vendor API; no cross-provider failover/routing.

status-page.comment: Google publishes a public Google Cloud service health / status dashboard covering its APIs. No dedicated status-page URL fetched in this pass.

no-training-default.comment: Paid tier: Google does not use prompts or responses to train/improve its products. The free/unpaid tier IS used to improve Google products (and may be human-reviewed) — value reflects the paid API default.

zero-data-retention.comment: Paid-tier prompts/responses are logged for a limited period for abuse/legal compliance; no zero-retention mode on the developer API. A configurable zero-data-retention path exists via Vertex AI.

compliance-certifications.comment: Gemini inherits Google's broad certification portfolio (SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001, ISO 42001) and GDPR/DPA coverage. HIPAA (BAA) and FedRAMP are surface-dependent and require Gemini via Vertex AI, not the standalone AI Studio API; corroborated by secondary sources, not fully confirmed from a single trust-center page for the developer API.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: API keys live in a Google Cloud project; org/console access can be governed via Google Cloud Identity / IAM and SSO at the project/console level.

pricing-model.comment: Per-token pay-as-you-go for paid usage, plus a free tier for development.

unified-billing.comment: One Google account/bill covers all reachable Gemini and Google model endpoints (single-vendor).

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch API is ~50% off standard rates; context caching is billed at a reduced input rate (up to ~90% lower for cached tokens) plus storage.

cheapest-entry.comment: A free tier is the lowest-friction way to start; pay-as-you-go follows.
